01_initial_request.target_name: Constitutive models for cyclic loading of sands
01_initial_request.input_data: Four advanced constitutive models (DM04, SANISAND-MSf, HP+IS, HP+ISA) and cyclic loading experimental test data on Toyoura and Karlsruhe fine sands
01_initial_request.user_intent: To show and discuss characteristic limitations shared by many constitutive models for cyclic loading of sands to guide future developments and applications
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Analysis and simulation of seven characteristic limitations including stress overshooting, one-way ratcheting, and liquefaction strength curves

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Constitutive models for sands under cyclic loading have progressed but share limitations. [Gap] A systematic analysis of these limitations across different model families is needed. [Decision] To achieve the goal of identifying characteristic limitations, chose to use Model Selection to perform selection of four advanced constitutive models (DM04, SANISAND-MSf, HP+IS, HP+ISA) on Toyoura and Karlsruhe fine sands, expecting to obtain a basis for comparative simulation analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Four models selected: two bounding surface elastoplasticity (DM04, SANISAND-MSf) and two hypoplasticity (HP+IS, HP+ISA). Experimental databases for Toyoura and Karlsruhe fine sands identified.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Overshooting after reverse loading is a known issue. [Gap] The extent of overshooting in HP+IS model needs quantification. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 1, chose to use HP+IS to perform simulation of undrained triaxial test with small strain unloading-reloading cycles on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain curves showing overshooting.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Simulations present reloading stress-strain response well above the reference monotonic curve. Overshooting decreases with increasing amplitude of reverse loading.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Bounding surface plasticity models also exhibit overshooting. [Gap] Performance of DM04 and SANISAND-MSf under cyclic triaxial tests needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 1, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of monotonic and cyclic triaxial tests on Toyoura sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain curves showing overshooting response.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Simulated stress-strain curve presents overshooting response caused by discrete memory variable updated at stress reversal.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SANISAND-MSf is an extension of DM04. [Gap] Whether SANISAND-MSf mitigates overshooting needs checking. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 1, chose to use SANISAND-MSf to perform simulation of monotonic and cyclic triaxial tests on Toyoura sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain curves showing overshooting response.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Simulated stress-strain curve presents overshooting response similar to DM04. Nature of bounding surface plasticity bounds the extent to an acceptable level.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] HP+ISA incorporates an elastic threshold strain. [Gap] Its performance on overshooting needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 1, chose to use HP+ISA to perform simulation of monotonic and cyclic triaxial tests on Toyoura sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain curves showing undershooting or overshooting.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Path after each loading cycle stays below the monotonic one, termed as 'undershooting'. Elastic threshold strain accounts for memory effects but remaining strain amplitude causes undershooting.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cyclic mobility involves shear strain accumulation. [Gap] DM04 capability to simulate two-way non-symmetric evolution of shear strain needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 2, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of undrained cyclic triaxial test on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain loops showing ratcheting.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Simulated stress-strain loops manifest unrealistic one-way ratcheting. Model lacks mechanisms for progressive reduction of shear stiffness at liquefaction state and balancing shear strain amplitudes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SANISAND-MSf introduces semifluidized state. [Gap] Its ability to reproduce increasing double amplitude of cyclic shear strains needs verification. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 2, chose to use SANISAND-MSf to perform simulation of undrained cyclic triaxial test on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain loops showing improved strain accumulation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model is able to reproduce the increasing double amplitude of cyclic shear strains. Mechanisms include reducing dilatancy/plastic modulus at low mean effective stresses and balancing compression/extension strain amplitudes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cyclic liquefaction strength curves (CSR-Nini) are crucial. [Gap] DM04 performance in capturing these curves needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 3, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of CSR-Nini curves for Karlsruhe fine sand at different densities, expecting to obtain liquefaction strength curves.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Simulations show much steeper strength curves than experiments for ru=0.95 and eps1_SA=2.5%. Simulations do not reach eps1_DA=5%.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SANISAND-MSf includes memory surface and semifluidized state. [Gap] Its performance on liquefaction strength curves needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 3, chose to use SANISAND-MSf to perform simulation of CSR-Nini curves for Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain improved liquefaction strength curves.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Significantly improved performance for all three criteria. Reaches eps1_DA=5% contrary to DM04. Slightly steeper curves for dense states.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Dense samples under large strain amplitude should reach liquefaction. [Gap] DM04 capability to simulate stress path attractor and stiffness degradation needs checking. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 4, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of undrained cyclic triaxial test on dense Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress paths showing liquefaction behavior.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model captures attractor p'=q=0 but stress path gets lockup after first loading cycle. Does not replicate reduction of q_amp as stress-strain loops do not degrade plastic modulus progressively.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SANISAND-MSf incorporates semifluidized state. [Gap] Its ability to capture progressive reduction of q_amp needs verification. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 4, chose to use SANISAND-MSf to perform simulation of undrained cyclic triaxial test on dense Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress paths showing stiffness degradation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model captures progressive reduction of q_amp and degradation of stress-strain loops to a nearly flat one after several loading cycles, comparable with experiment.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multidimensional cyclic loading on closed stress loops should show elastic response. [Gap] DM04 performance on plastic accumulation in q-p' closed loops needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 5, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of multidimensional stress path (circle in Q-P space) on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain strain accumulation data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Simulation exhibits significant accumulation of plastic strain and noticeable contraction. Inability to deactivate plastic strain rate due to small yield surface size and plastic modulus parameters.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] HP+ISA incorporates a bubble shaped elastic locus. [Gap] Its performance on closed loop cycles needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 5, chose to use HP+ISA to perform simulation of multidimensional stress path on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain strain accumulation data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Only model able to deactivate completely the plastic strain rate under such path. Still produces very small plastic strain rate but remains most successful.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cyclic oedometer tests are important for settlement predictions. [Gap] DM04 performance on oedometric stiffness needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 6, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of cyclic oedometer test on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain response.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model shows generally stiffer behavior. Open ended narrow cone-type yield surface does not generate plasticity under constant stress ratio loading.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hypoplastic models consider void ratio characteristic loading curves. [Gap] HP+IS performance on oedometric reloading paths needs checking. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 6, chose to use HP+IS to perform simulation of cyclic oedometer test on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain response.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model performs better in regard to stiffness but simulates wrong in reloading paths. Overshooting is observed on the HP+IS model for all cases.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Drained preloading affects subsequent undrained shearing. [Gap] SANISAND-MSf capability to capture this effect needs verification. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 7, chose to use SANISAND-MSf to perform simulation of undrained triaxial test with drained preloading on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain response showing preloading influence.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model reproduces the influence of the deviatoric preloading due to the introduction of memory surface, whose evolution during preloading increases stiffness.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] DM04 lacks memory effects on cycles of larger amplitudes. [Gap] Its performance on drained preloading effect needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve the goal of analyzing Limitation 7, chose to use DM04 to perform simulation of undrained triaxial test with drained preloading on Karlsruhe fine sand, expecting to obtain stress-strain response.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model has a similar effect as HP+ISA. Yield surface is narrow and lacks memory effects on cycles of larger amplitudes, failing to capture the strong influence of drained preloading.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparison of numerical simulation results from four constitutive models (DM04, SANISAND-MSf, HP+IS, HP+ISA) against laboratory experimental test data on Toyoura and Karlsruhe fine sands.

limitations_identified.interpretation: Seven characteristic limitations were successfully identified and discussed.

models_analyzed.interpretation: Four advanced constitutive models were evaluated.

overshooting_observed.interpretation: Overshooting phenomenon confirmed in DM04, SANISAND-MSf, and HP+IS; undershooting in HP+ISA.

liquefaction_curve_accuracy.interpretation: SANISAND-MSf showed significantly improved performance in CSR-Nini curves compared to DM04.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully showed and discussed seven characteristic limitations shared by many constitutive models for cyclic loading of sands, providing guidance for future developments and applications.
